meta.description: Unified causal graph encoding financial market mental models as a dense network of concepts, edges, feedback loops, chains, regimes, axioms, and forks.

concepts.label: Credit Impulse (Delta-Delta Credit)
concepts.description: Second derivative of total debt creation (public + private). Drives nominal demand, profits, and asset prices. Impulse matters, not level.

concepts.label: Private Credit Growth
concepts.description: Bank lending expansion. ~3x GDP multiplier vs public deficit. Demand-driven, constrained by capital ratios not reserves.

concepts.label: Government Deficit Spending
concepts.description: Fiscal deficit creates private savings via sectoral balance identity. The engine of nominal growth when private credit is flat.

concepts.label: Nominal Demand
concepts.description: Total nominal spending in the economy. Downstream of credit impulse with ~4-6 quarter lag.

concepts.label: Corporate Profits
concepts.description: Proportional to total debt (Kalecki: Pi ~ 3.5% x Total Debt). A balance-sheet variable, not a productivity variable.

concepts.label: Asset Prices
concepts.description: Equity and real estate valuations. Driven by credit impulse, wealth effect, and risk premiums.

concepts.label: Collateral Values
concepts.description: Value of assets pledged against loans. Rising collateral enables more lending (reflexive loop).

concepts.label: Debt/GDP Ratio (Omega)
concepts.description: Stock of debt relative to income. AR(1) persistence of 0.96. The missing state variable in macro.

concepts.label: Debt Saturation Threshold
concepts.description: Level of Omega where marginal debt stops generating growth. Japan is the completed experiment.

concepts.label: Endogenous Money Creation
concepts.description: Loans create deposits simultaneously. Banks do not lend reserves. Credit is the active monetary variable; M2 is a residual.

concepts.label: Bank Lending Standards (SLOOS)
concepts.description: Senior Loan Officer Survey. Leads credit growth by ~12 months. Tightening precedes slowdown.

concepts.label: Debt Service Ratio
concepts.description: Interest payments as share of income. Must stay in tolerable band (~10-15%). Forces yields down when Omega rises.

concepts.label: Treasury General Account Balance
concepts.description: Treasury's checking account at the Fed. Dynamic risk policy floor based on 1-week forward outflows. Thermostat mechanism.

concepts.label: Treasury Bill Issuance
concepts.description: Fast tactical lever adjusted weekly. Shock absorber for fiscal surprises. Bills as % of total debt fluctuates.

concepts.label: Treasury Coupon Issuance
concepts.description: Slow policy lever changed at quarterly refunding. Structural signal of deficit growth. Drives duration supply.

concepts.label: Bills vs Coupons Gate
concepts.description: Whether deficit is funded via bills (stimulative, cash-equivalent) or coupons (restrictive, duration risk). The most impactful fiscal decision.

concepts.label: Quarterly Refunding Announcement
concepts.description: Deterministic once financing need identity known. Coupon trajectory + TGA target + bill residual.

concepts.label: Extraordinary Measures (Debt Ceiling)
concepts.description: G-Fund suspension (~$300B capacity). Depletes daily until X-date. Buffer-until-break pattern.

concepts.label: Reverse Repo (RRP) Balance
concepts.description: MMF cash parked at Fed overnight. Drains when bill yields exceed RRP rate. Structural floor exists.

concepts.label: Bank Reserves
concepts.description: Aggregate reserves at Fed. Distribution matters more than level -- pockets of scarcity cause stress.

concepts.label: Money Market Fund Allocation
concepts.description: MMFs choose between bills, RRP, private repo, FHLB floaters based on yield spread and WAM constraints.

concepts.label: Repo Market Rates
concepts.description: Collateralized lending rates. GC tracks Fed Funds. Specials reflect specific security demand. Financing cost for all positions.

concepts.label: Federal Funds Rate
concepts.description: Overnight interbank rate. Set within Fed corridor. FHLB-FBO-IORB arbitrage chain keeps it in range.

concepts.label: Interest on Reserve Balances
concepts.description: Rate Fed pays on reserves. Ceiling-adjacent rate. When Fed pays more than portfolio yield, small-scale money printing.

concepts.label: Dealer Balance Sheet Capacity
concepts.description: SLR and leverage ratio constrain dealer intermediation. The binding constraint on market liquidity. Nettable vs unnettable repos matter.

concepts.label: Fed Rate Corridor (Floor-Ceiling)
concepts.description: RRP floor, IORB near-ceiling, SRF/DW ceiling. Self-correcting: rates above ceiling trigger facility usage, below floor trigger RRP absorption.

concepts.label: Settlement / Fedwire Mechanics
concepts.description: DVP closes 3:30 PM, triparty 3:30-6:00 PM. Timing mismatches create daylight overdrafts. BNY is sole triparty agent.

concepts.label: Quarter-End Window Dressing
concepts.description: Banks shrink balance sheets for reporting. Shift from unnettable to nettable repos. Predictable rate spikes at quarter/year-end.

concepts.label: FHLB Hidden Connector
concepts.description: FHLBs dont earn IORB, so lend via fed funds to foreign banks who arbitrage. Primary mechanism keeping EFFR in target range.

concepts.label: Standing Repo Facility (SRF)
concepts.description: Rate ceiling enforcement. Stigma limits usage. TOMOs (no stigma) used as alternative. Rate cap mechanism.

concepts.label: FX Swap / Dollar Funding
concepts.description: Dollar funding for foreign banks via basis swaps and central bank swap lines. Cross-border plumbing stress channel.

concepts.label: Hedge Fund Leverage via Repo
concepts.description: Cash to repo to treasury to repo to treasury. Leverage stacking via sponsored repo. Basis trade growth.

concepts.label: Tax Withholding Receipts
concepts.description: Real-time labor market signal. Daily DTS data faster than BLS. Core 20 business day baseline with seasonal overlays.

concepts.label: Seasonal Treasury Cash Flows
concepts.description: April 15 tax surge, SS payment schedule, GSE remittance cycle, quarter-end flows. Predictable periodic patterns.

concepts.label: Term Premium
concepts.description: Compensation for bearing duration risk beyond rate expectations. Supply-demand determined. The actual stance of financial conditions.

concepts.label: Risk Premium
concepts.description: Price of risk across asset classes. Low risk premiums stimulate regardless of rate levels.

concepts.label: Financial Conditions
concepts.description: Composite of rates, spreads, equity prices, dollar. Term premium, not fed funds, determines actual tightness.

concepts.label: Wealth Effect
concepts.description: Top 10% of households drive ~50% of consumption. Equity valuations transmit financial conditions to real economy.

concepts.label: Equity Multiples (P/E)
concepts.description: Inversely related to long real yields. Certainty premiums (government backstops) justify structurally higher multiples.

concepts.label: Long-End Yields (10Y+)
concepts.description: Composite: expectations + term premium + convexity bias. The bond market is everything -- all assets price off the risk-free long rate.

concepts.label: Short-End Rates
concepts.description: Governed by CB policy path. 2Y yield = 24-month rolling window of monetary policy expectations.

concepts.label: Inflation
concepts.description: Lagged accounting cascade: Credit -> Assets -> Prices -> Wages. House prices lead shelter CPI by 16 months.

concepts.label: Inflation Expectations
concepts.description: 5Y5Y forward reacts to, never propels, the cycle. Wages follow prices adaptively (1Q lag), not expectations.

concepts.label: Fed Reaction Function
concepts.description: Dual mandate pendulum between inflation and employment. Committee divergence brakes speed of repricing. Political actor constrained by incentives.

concepts.label: Fiscal Dominance
concepts.description: When debt/GDP is high, fiscal flows dominate monetary policy. Deficit is the engine, rates are the steering wheel.

concepts.label: Fear Policy / Foam the Runway
concepts.description: Policymakers always intervene to prevent disorderly bond market. Balancing loop that fights the correction the economy needs.

concepts.label: Income-Driven vs Credit-Driven Economy
concepts.description: Post-Covid economy is income-driven (wages + wealth). Short rates ineffective because fixed-rate mortgages insulate. Need long yields to slow demand.

concepts.label: Spot Money vs Future Money
concepts.description: Protecting bonds (future money) devalues currency (spot money). Cannot save both simultaneously.

concepts.label: Savings-Investment Balance
concepts.description: Determines sensitivity of long yields to public debt. AI capex and Chinas surplus are the two largest structural forces.

concepts.label: Rate Tail Distribution
concepts.description: Right tail (higher yields) vs left tail (lower yields). Distribution shape matters more than point forecast. Inflation is the gatekeeper.

concepts.label: Tariff Transmission
concepts.description: Either price pass-through (inflationary but reshores) or foreign margin absorption (non-inflationary but doesnt reshore). Successful tariffs require some inflation.

concepts.label: Uncertainty as Economic Damage
concepts.description: Uncertainty itself freezes hiring and capex even when policy doesnt fully materialize. No hire, no fire stall speed.

concepts.label: Fed-Treasury Coordination
concepts.description: Bill-heavy issuance + Fed RMPs = implicit monetization. Stealth Operation Twist. Both institutions want lower long-end yields.

concepts.label: AI Disinflation Paradox
concepts.description: AI is structurally disinflationary (productivity) but short-run reflationary (wealth effect from AI equity valuations). Paradox of coexisting forces.

concepts.label: Claim Dilution
concepts.description: When new debt exceeds productive capacity expansion, excess nominal claims reconciled by rising prices. Unifies credit-to-inflation transmission.

concepts.label: Implied Volatility
concepts.description: Market price of expected future volatility. Systematically exceeds realized vol (vol risk premium). Encodes distribution beliefs.

concepts.label: Realized Volatility
concepts.description: Actual observed price variation. Hedging frequency determines which realized vol you sample. Zero-mean vs standard matters.

concepts.label: Gamma Exposure (GEX)
concepts.description: Net gamma of dealer hedging book. Positive GEX stabilizes (dealers buy dips, sell rips). Near zero = neutral. Negative = destabilizing.

concepts.label: Vanna Exposure (VEX)
concepts.description: Dealer vanna exposure. When puts go ITM, rising IV forces dealers to sell underlying. Self-reinforcing crash mechanism.

concepts.label: Gamma-Theta Contest
concepts.description: Daily PnL = 0.5*Gamma*(Move)^2 - Theta*dt. If realized > implied, long gamma wins. Fundamental options trade-off.

concepts.label: Volatility Surface
concepts.description: Strike x expiry grid of implied vols. Skew = spot-vol correlation belief. Term structure = mean-reversion belief. Sticky strike vs floating regimes.

concepts.label: Volatility Risk Premium (VRP)
concepts.description: IV systematically exceeds RV. Sellers earn premium for bearing tail risk. Not free money -- compensates for catastrophic loss potential.

concepts.label: Dispersion / Correlation
concepts.description: Index vol = avg stock vol x avg correlation. Crashes: both vol AND correlation spike (multiplicative). Basket of options > option on basket.

concepts.label: Volatility Drain
concepts.description: Geometric returns < arithmetic by 0.5*variance. High vol destructive to compounded wealth. Explains why long vol loses ~58% of the time at fair pricing.

concepts.label: Earnings Volatility Extraction
concepts.description: Isolate event vol from term structure. IV increases as earnings approach. True theta much less than model theta until event day.

concepts.label: Put-Call Parity Identity
concepts.description: C = (S-K) + P + cost_of_carry. Covered call = naked put. Connects every concept in options. Deepest single relationship.

concepts.label: Risk-Neutral Pricing
concepts.description: Option price = expected payoff under risk-neutral measure. Not real-world probability. Best-capitalized, most-diversified player sets the marginal price.

concepts.label: Dealer Positioning
concepts.description: Dealer delta-hedging obligations form hidden liquidity layer. The implied order book dominates visible limit order book.

concepts.label: Consensus / Crowded Positioning
concepts.description: When positioning reaches extremes, highest-probability move inflicts maximum pain. Fragile equilibria resolve violently.

concepts.label: Narrative Cycle
concepts.description: Narratives persist -> consensus aligns -> confirming data arrives -> if price doesnt respond = exhaustion signal -> reversal.

concepts.label: Good News / Bad Price Signal
concepts.description: When positive news fails to produce positive price, market signals narrative exhaustion and imminent reversal. Mirror: bad news / good price at bottoms.

concepts.label: Pain Trade Engine
concepts.description: Crowded trades create fragile equilibria. Small catalyst -> violent unwind in direction of maximum pain. Positioning extremes are the precondition.

concepts.label: VAR Shock / Crowded Unwind
concepts.description: Volatility events target most crowded positions. Uniform positioning -> fragile equilibrium -> any catalyst -> liquidation cascade.

concepts.label: Correlation Break as Regime Signal
concepts.description: When a correlation that should hold doesnt (e.g. USD not rallying on tariffs), something structural has changed. Earliest observable regime-change tell.

concepts.label: Price Action Over Narrative
concepts.description: When price and narrative diverge, trust price. What matters is not WHY but WHAT the market is doing.

concepts.label: Survey Data Structural Break
concepts.description: Post-COVID: inflation-sentiment contamination, partisan response bias, negativity bias. Hard data outranks soft data.

concepts.label: Event Volatility Premium
concepts.description: Markets systematically overprice known-event vol. Realized move rarely exceeds implied breakeven. Fade front-end.

concepts.label: Current Account Balance
concepts.description: CA deficit = capital inflow (identity). US as 60% of global deficit = structural demand for US assets from surplus countries.

concepts.label: Cross-Border Capital Flows
concepts.description: Post-GFC shift: bank paper -> equities. Composition determines which US assets benefit. Foreign CBs diversifying away from USTs.

concepts.label: Commodity Prices
concepts.description: Downstream of credit cycle via stocks -> dollar -> oil chain. Causality runs from commodities to dollar, not reverse (consensus has it backward).

concepts.label: US Dollar
concepts.description: Master cross-asset regime variable. Dollar/yield decoupling signals regime change. Currencies are shares of global production (GDP-FX parity).

concepts.label: Surplus Country Trap
concepts.description: High debt + CA surplus = trapped. Must maintain surplus or face sudden stop. Japan and China cannot easily pivot. NIIP buffer is finite.

concepts.label: BOP / Sectoral Balance Identity
concepts.description: Private balance + government balance + foreign balance = 0. One sectors deficit is mechanically anothers surplus.

concepts.label: Commodity-Dollar Cycle (10yr)
concepts.description: Commodities up -> EM earnings -> capital to EM -> USD down -> commodities up (reinforcing). Reversal: vicious cycle in other direction.

concepts.label: Mutually Assured Economic Destruction
concepts.description: US (deficit/consumer) and China (surplus/producer) locked in interdependence. Debt = fissile material, tariffs = nuclear button.

concepts.label: Flight-to-Quality Status
concepts.description: Whether Treasuries retain safe-haven status. Tested at each risk-off episode. Negative equity-Treasury correlation = intact. Positive = impaired.

concepts.label: Exchange Rates
concepts.description: Piecewise cointegrated with relative real GDP. GDP-FX parity: currencies price relative economic equity. Credit drives FX through GDP and assets.

concepts.label: Crisis vs Panic Discriminator
concepts.description: Panics: positioning flush + no credit deterioration = buy the dip. Crises: credit supply freeze + secured lending losses = defend. Most drawdowns are panics.

concepts.label: Credit Supply Freeze
concepts.description: The crisis signature. Not demand decline but supply freeze. Unexpected losses on safe secured lending spike provisions 50-100x.

concepts.label: Provisioning Spike in Secured Lending
concepts.description: Losses in secured (not unsecured) lending cause system shock. Each crisis originates in a NEW safe asset class.

concepts.label: Plumbing / Funding Stress
concepts.description: Reserve drain hits pockets of scarcity. Repo rate spikes, forced selling, independent of economic deterioration. Sept 2019 template.

concepts.label: Business Cycle Suppression / Death
concepts.description: Emergency tools normalized -> vol crushed -> risk premiums compressed -> crowding -> violent blow-up -> reset. ~2yr calm cadence.

concepts.label: Minsky Financial Instability
concepts.description: Hedge -> speculative -> Ponzi finance. Stability breeds instability. Period of calm builds leverage that makes eventual correction violent.

concepts.label: Dampener Exhaustion
concepts.description: Stabilizer has finite capacity, then becomes accelerant. Partial claims suppress DLQ until exhausted. Emergency tools suppress vol until crowding.

concepts.label: Money Printing Detection
concepts.description: Requires: (1) CB equity depletion (unbacked) AND (2) spendable money creation. QE fails both tests (asset swap). Fiscal deficits pass test 2.

concepts.label: Bond Price-Yield Convexity
concepts.description: Price gains from yield drops exceed losses from equal yield rises. Convexity is valuable. Second-order effect dominates in large moves.

concepts.label: Carry and Rolldown
concepts.description: Holding return = coupon carry + rolldown as curve ages position. Carry-to-volatility ratio is the proper risk-adjusted measure.

concepts.label: Duration as Sensitivity
concepts.description: Modified duration translates yield changes to price changes. DV01 normalizes across instruments. Yield betas adjust for cross-sector hedging.

concepts.label: Swap Spread Decomposition
concepts.description: Swap rate minus Treasury yield = credit + funding + supply/demand. Mortgage hedging is the most powerful short-term driver.

concepts.label: Mortgage Negative Convexity Feedback
concepts.description: Rates rise -> prepayments fall -> MBS duration extends -> hedgers sell -> rates rise more. Self-reinforcing spiral. The dominant flow in rates.

concepts.label: Treasury Futures Basis / CTD
concepts.description: Basis = cash - futures*CF. Net basis = delivery option value. CTD switches at 6% pivot. Futures systematically cheap by option premium.

concepts.label: Supply/Demand for Duration
concepts.description: Foreign CBs buy intermediate. Pensions buy long-end. Banks buy front. Hedge funds lever via basis trade. Structural shifts move specific curve sectors.

concepts.label: Yield Curve Shape
concepts.description: PCA: level (~85%), slope (~10%), curvature (~3%). Bull steep = Fed cuts. Bear flat = Fed hikes. Curve is directional trade when terminal anchored.

concepts.label: Rate Options / Swaptions
concepts.description: Add volatility dimension to rates. Gamma/theta fundamental trade-off. Conditional trades isolate desired exposure. Vol surface in rates encodes dominant risk.

concepts.label: On-the-Run / Off-the-Run Spread
concepts.description: Most recently issued = most liquid, often on special in repo. Older issues trade cheap. Spread = liquidity premium.

concepts.label: Treasury Auction Demand
concepts.description: Stop-through/tail, dealer allocation, end-user participation. Auctions bring out buyers. Higher yields -> stronger sponsorship -> self-correcting.

concepts.label: Yield Range Consolidation
concepts.description: 10Y oscillates within range until catalyst breaks out. Bollinger band compression signals imminent move. New range forms at shifted level.

concepts.label: Implied Equity Risk Premium
concepts.description: Solved backward from market prices, cash flows, growth expectations. Best single gauge of market valuation. Far superior to PE ratios.

concepts.label: DCF / Intrinsic Value
concepts.description: PV of expected cash flows. Four inputs: revenue growth, margins, reinvestment, risk. Earnings are not cash flows.

concepts.label: Pricing vs Valuation
concepts.description: Pricing = what others will pay (multiples). Valuation = what something is worth (cash flows). Different questions, can give opposite signals.

concepts.label: Competitive Advantage Decay
concepts.description: Excess returns exist only with moats. Width (ROIC-WACC) and depth (duration). Competition relentlessly erodes. 70%+ of firms earn below cost of capital.

concepts.label: Corporate Life Cycle
concepts.description: Startup -> growth -> maturity -> decline. Tools, capital structure, risk measures shift at each stage. Nobody wants to get old.

concepts.label: Big Market Delusion
concepts.description: Big new market -> overconfident entrepreneurs + VCs -> individually plausible, collectively fairy tale. Sum of break-even revenues > TAM.

concepts.label: Break-Even Revenue Analysis
concepts.description: Reverse-engineer from market cap what revenue/margins must be delivered. Apply 3P test (possible, plausible, probable).

concepts.label: Equity as Call Option on Assets
concepts.description: Equity = max(V-D, 0). Time and volatility keep equity alive when intrinsic = 0. Distressed equity pricing, wealth transfer to equity from debt.

concepts.label: Gold as Catastrophe Insurance
concepts.description: Collectible, not asset. Cannot be valued, only priced. Weak hedge against normal inflation. Strong hedge against hyperinflation/catastrophe.

concepts.label: AI Competition Trap
concepts.description: AI makes companies productive but not profitable collectively. If everybody has it, nobody has it. Competition passes savings to consumers as lower prices.

concepts.label: Stock-Bond Correlation Regime
concepts.description: Depends on CB attitude toward inflation tail, not rate levels. Negative correlation (60/40 works) only when CB unconstrained. Not physics, just a regime.

concepts.label: Ouroboros (Vol Reflexivity Loop)
concepts.description: ~$2T of financial engineering strategies simultaneously drive and are driven by vol. Lower vol feeds lower vol until exogenous shock reverses violently.

concepts.label: Short Volatility Crowding
concepts.description: Modern institutional portfolio is ~98% short vol and doesnt know it. Covered calls, risk parity, vol targeting -- all secretly the same bet.

concepts.label: Regime Diversification
concepts.description: Diversify across macro regimes (growth, deflation, inflation, squeeze), not across asset classes that are secretly the same short-vol bet.

concepts.label: Risk Transmutation (Not Destruction)
concepts.description: CB suppression transmutes risk from price mechanism to hidden tail risk and social instability. The longer suppression, the larger eventual realization.

concepts.label: CapEx Inherent Cyclicality
concepts.description: Every this-time-is-different capex boom ends in a glut. AI capex follows the same arc. Picks and shovels work until they dont.

concepts.label: Win Rate vs Expectancy
concepts.description: High win rate does not equal positive expectancy. Short vol wins most months but loses catastrophically. Slugging percentage matters, not batting average.

concepts.label: Housing -> Shelter CPI Pipeline
concepts.description: House prices lead shelter CPI by 16 months. Shelter = 43% of Core CPI. Credit-to-shelter channel is the dominant inflation transmission mechanism.

concepts.label: Wages Follow Prices (Adaptive)
concepts.description: Wages follow prices with 1-quarter lag, virtually deterministically. Not forward-looking. Expectations play decorative, not causal role.

concepts.label: CPI Measurement Critique
concepts.description: CPI mixes domestic and import prices. Wrong inflation target. GDP deflator adjusted for terms of trade is correct domestic inflation measure.

concepts.label: FHA Partial Claims Exhaustion
concepts.description: Loss-absorption buffer suppresses headline DLQ. 30% UPB cap. When exhausted, loans re-default, hidden distress becomes visible. Dampener exhaustion pattern.

concepts.label: New Money Tracker
concepts.description: TGA drawdown + bank loan creation. Short-term liquidity signal. MA rising + above threshold = long equities.

concepts.label: TIPS as Broken Signal
concepts.description: Dominated by liquidity premiums and structural flow, not inflation expectations. Equity multiples and gold provide cleaner real-rate signals.

concepts.label: Financing Constraint Ceiling
concepts.description: Dealer balance sheet -> equity financing cost vs SOFR -> hard ceiling on risk appetite. Rally cap regardless of fundamentals.

concepts.label: Cross-Market Rate Spillovers
concepts.description: Japan is primary exporter of bearish long-end shocks. JGB moves transmit 2-3bp per 10bp shock to US/DE/UK. Global long-end views must incorporate Japan.

concepts.label: VVIX/VIX Bible (Vol-of-Vol Leads)
concepts.description: Composed indicator: vol-of-vol (VVIX) leads implied vol (VIX) which leads spot. When VVIX diverges from VIX, a regime shift in vol is approaching.

concepts.label: FX Rate-Residual Mean Reversion
concepts.description: Regression-based: FX vs rate differential. When residual (FX after controlling for rates) hits extreme Z-score, mean-reverts. Isolates the non-rate component of FX.

concepts.label: Quarter-End Collar Roll (JHEQX)
concepts.description: JPM hedged equity fund rolls a massive SPX collar quarterly. Creates periodic inelastic buy/sell flow at predictable strikes. Local turning points around roll dates.

concepts.label: Foreign Capital Diversification / Exit
concepts.description: Sanctions, protectionism, or reserve diversification trigger foreign selling of USTs. Yields up + USD down simultaneously. Structural shift in who finances the deficit.

concepts.label: Levered ETF Option Distortion
concepts.description: Options on 2x ETFs cannot be naively priced at 2x vol. OTM calls worth MORE than 2x (vega convexity), OTM puts LESS. Rebalancing mechanics create vol drag and pricing artifacts.

concepts.label: Value of Control
concepts.description: Every company has status quo value and optimal value. The gap, weighted by probability of management change, is the control premium. Voting vs non-voting spread reflects this. Badly-run mature companies have highest control value.

concepts.label: Acquisition Value Destruction
concepts.description: 55-83% of acquisitions destroy value. Risk transference, debt subsidies, phantom synergies, control premium overpayment. Target shareholders capture all value. Addiction sets in.

concepts.label: Passive Investing Flow Amplification
concepts.description: Passive dominance removes volatility buffers. Each incremental buyer/seller amplifies tail risk. Markets become entirely flow-driven. The drunk friend wanders off the cliff.

concepts.label: Credit-Vol Brotherhood
concepts.description: Credit regime shifts drive volatility regime shifts. Vol is the brother of credit. To forecast vol, track credit conditions and solvency, not vol itself.

concepts.label: Centrally Cleared Treasury Market
concepts.description: FICC netting provides balance sheet relief for dealers. Shifts who can intermediate. Sponsored repo via FICC allows hedge funds direct access. Changes the liquidity topology.

concepts.label: Short Vol Pyramid (3-Layer Cascade)
concepts.description: Explicit ($60B: VIX ETPs, vol selling HFs, pension overwriting) -> Implicit ($1.4T: risk parity, VAR control, risk premia) -> Structural ($3.8T: share buybacks). Cascade sequence: explicit blows first, implicit next, structural last.

concepts.label: CB Preemptive Strike / Pavlovian Moral Hazard
concepts.description: Post-Draghi shift from reactive to preemptive. Market dip 5% -> CB signals/acts -> rebound -> moral hazard accumulates. Risk not destroyed, transmuted to populism and hidden tail events.

concepts.label: Dragon Portfolio (100-Year Regime Construction)
concepts.description: Equal thematic weight across secular growth, deflation/crisis, inflation/stagflation. Hawk + Serpent = Dragon. Lever to vol target. Do NOT predict regime. 60/40 covers 2 regimes; Dragon covers all.

concepts.label: Hawk and Serpent Regime Archetypes
concepts.description: Serpent: secular growth, moral hazard, reflexive self-cannibalization (the Ouroboros). Hawk: secular change via inflation (right wing) or deflation (left wing). Serpent can last decades; Hawk arrivals are violent.

concepts.label: George Lucas Optionality (Carry for Convexity)
concepts.description: Sell first-order move (linear carry, term structure roll) to finance higher-order convexity (VIX>50, 20%+ decline). Lucas sold $850K salary, bought merchandising rights worth $45B. Makes long vol carry-efficient.

concepts.label: Forest Fire Ignition Model
concepts.description: Monitor conditions (leverage, short-vol crowding, liquidity, valuations), not sparks (catalysts). Conditions determine if any spark becomes conflagration. Pattern match across 1928/1987/1998/2008.

concepts.label: Vol Persistence Regime (Low vs High)
concepts.description: Low persistence (modern): vol spikes -> CB rescues -> mean reverts. Short vol works. High persistence (1930s/1970s): vol spikes -> CB constrained -> stays elevated for YEARS. Long vol becomes positive carry. Inflation is the regime switch.

concepts.label: US Exceptionalism Cycle (TINA vs Sell America)
concepts.description: Long cycle: US relative growth -> capital flows to US -> USD up + US equity outperformance -> TINA entrenches -> positioning extreme -> trigger -> reversal to Sell America. Reflexive.

concepts.label: Falling Maple Seed (Nonlinear Inflation Descent)
concepts.description: Inflation descends nonlinearly with updrafts from sticky components. Goods deflation fast, services viscous like peanut butter, shelter lagged 16mo. Each updraft (tariffs, shelter, insurance) frustrates but doesnt reverse the trend.

concepts.label: Sentiment Polarity (Regime-Dependent Contrarianism)
concepts.description: Extreme positioning is contrarian in mean-reverting assets (GBP, CAD) but continuation in trending assets (USDJPY, equities in bull). Must classify asset as mean-reverting or trending BEFORE applying sentiment signal.

concepts.label: Crypto as Risk Canary
concepts.description: Bitcoin/crypto leads risk appetite. No fundamental valuation constraint -> pure positioning. Crypto turns lower while QQQ chugs -> equities follow with lag. Shitcoin rallies = peak retail FOMO.

concepts.label: CB Policy Divergence Engine (G4+)
concepts.description: Structural growth/inflation mix differences across Fed/ECB/BOE/BOJ/BOC create exploitable cross-market rate spreads. Front-end divergence persists (domestic CB path); long-end divergence bounded (global risk premium correlated).

concepts.label: Curve Segmentation Machine (Front/Belly/Long)
concepts.description: Three distinct machines: front-end (CB policy path), belly (terminal rate + convexity), long-end (risk premium + supply + cross-border). A single macro factor rarely moves the whole curve. Curve trades are decomposition bets.

concepts.label: Global Divergence / Weak Link
concepts.description: US fixed-rate mortgages vs variable-rate economies (UK, AUS, Canada, Nordics). Rate sensitivity diverges massively. CB divergence trade: weak-link economies crack first, transmit back to US via FX and trade.

edges.description: Credit acceleration drives nominal spending with multi-quarter lag
Kalecki: profits proportional to total debt creation
Credit acceleration lifts valuations through demand and collateral channels
Rising assets improve collateral available for lending
Better collateral enables banks to extend more credit
Private credit expansion feeds into the aggregate credit impulse
Fiscal deficit contributes to total credit impulse (but ~1/3 the multiplier of private)
Government spending directly creates demand
SLOOS tightening leads credit contraction by ~12 months
As Omega rises toward threshold, marginal debt effectiveness declines
Beyond saturation, more credit lowers growth (negative marginal returns)
Higher Omega forces yields down via debt service constraint and DCF anchor
DSR must stay tolerable; if Omega rises, rates MUST fall
Loans create deposits; credit is demand-driven, not reserve-constrained
Credit impulse drives inflation through lagged accounting cascade
Credit drives house prices which lead shelter CPI by 16 months
Shelter = 43% of core CPI; house prices lead by 16 months
Wages follow prices adaptively with 1-quarter lag
Excess claims from unproductive debt must be reconciled by rising prices
Profits lead imports by 2 quarters; imports track GDP
TGA below floor triggers bill supply increase (thermostat)
New bill issuance refills TGA
Bill supply rise -> yields above RRP -> MMFs buy bills -> RRP drains
MMF yield comparison determines where cash parks: bills, repo, or RRP
Debt ceiling binding disables bill issuance thermostat
EM depletion drains TGA toward zero when ceiling binds
Withholding and tax day receipts are the deposit side of TGA
Predictable periodic flows create TGA swings
Coupons add duration supply; bills do not. Coupon-heavy issuance raises term premium
Bills = stimulative (cash equivalent); coupons = restrictive (duration risk)
Reserve scarcity (distributed, not aggregate) triggers funding stress
SLR constrains dealer capacity to intermediate; when full, rates spike
Quarter-end balance sheet shrinkage causes predictable rate spikes
RRP floor and SRF ceiling bound the federal funds rate
FHLB-FBO-IORB arbitrage chain keeps EFFR within target range
Leveraged basis trade growth compresses basis, cheapens futures
Funding stress forces selling independent of economic conditions
RRP drain preserves reserves; if bills funded from reserves instead, scarcity results
Term premium is a direct component of long-end yields
Higher long real rate compresses P/E multiples
Higher multiples directly raise equity valuations
Equity and real estate prices drive consumption via top 10%
Top 10% drives ~50% of consumption; wealth effect is the transmission
Demand drives prices with variable lag depending on slack
Rising inflation shifts Fed toward tightening mandate
Fed tightening raises short-end rates
Short rates slow demand in credit-driven economies but NOT income-driven ones
If economy is income-driven, short rates are ineffective; need long yields to slow demand
When debt/GDP high, issuance composition dominates monetary policy
Policy intervention prevents disorderly long-end selloff; keeps term premium compressed
Coupon supply vs buyer demand clears via term premium adjustment
When investment rises relative to savings, yield sensitivity to debt increases
Protecting bonds devalues currency; protecting currency crashes bonds
Tail shape determines optimal option structure; own convexity when tails mispriced
Tariffs cause one-time price adjustment; not demand-driven if contained to goods
Uncertainty freezes hiring and capex even before policy materializes
Bill-heavy issuance + Fed RMPs = stealth Operation Twist compressing long-end
JGB moves transmit 2-3bp per 10bp shock to global long-end yields
AI productivity gains are structurally disinflationary over time
AI equity valuations support consumption via wealth effect in the near term
Positive GEX stabilizes: dealers buy dips, sell rips, compressing realized vol
Falling realized vol pulls down implied vol
Higher IV compresses gamma toward zero; GEX weakens
Negative VEX forces dealer selling, creating self-reinforcing crash loop
Rising IV causes vanna-driven selling that destabilizes markets
VRP is the systematic tendency for implied to exceed realized; gamma-theta contest is the daily expression
Index vol embeds both component vol and correlation; steep skew reflects crash correlation dynamics
Geometric < arithmetic by 0.5*variance; high vol destructive to compounded wealth
Known events systematically overpriced in IV; fade front-end around scheduled events
Dealer hedging obligations form hidden liquidity layer; the implied order book
Put-call parity connects all option prices; cost of carry embedded in the surface
Option prices set by risk-neutral measure; best-capitalized player sets marginal price
Extreme positioning creates fragile equilibrium; pain trade probability rises
Narrative exhaustion manifests as good news failing to produce positive price action
Crowded positioning sets up liquidation cascade when catalyst hits
Forced liquidation spikes realized vol
Correlation break is the earliest observable signal of regime change
When price and narrative diverge, trust price; narrative eventually capitulates
CA deficit = capital inflow. US as 60% of global deficit = structural asset demand.
Post-GFC shift from bank paper to equities explains S&P outperformance
Commodities drive dollar (not reverse). Commodity up -> EM earnings -> capital to EM -> USD down
Weak dollar raises dollar-denominated commodity prices (reinforcing loop)
Credit drives FX indirectly: credit -> GDP -> asset prices -> FX
Surplus countries must export capital; cannot easily pivot to consumption-led growth
Private + government + foreign balance = 0; deficit spending creates private savings
US-China interdependence makes tariffs the nuclear button; escalation risks mutual recession
Risk-off with negative equity-Treasury correlation pushes yields down
10-year reinforcing cycle: commodities -> EM earnings -> flows -> USD -> commodities
Credit supply freezing = crisis (defend). No freeze = panic (buy the dip).
50-100x provisioning spike in secured lending triggers credit supply freeze
Reserve scarcity pockets spike repo rates, forcing asset selling
Emergency tools normalize -> vol crushed -> short-vol strategies accumulate
Crowded short-vol creates fragile equilibrium; violent blow-up when catalyst arrives
Ponzi finance collapse triggers forced deleveraging and credit contraction
Exhausted stabilizers convert hidden risk into visible crisis
PC usage accumulates against 30% cap; when exhausted, DLQ spikes 50-100x
CB suppression transmutes risk into hidden tail; longer suppression = larger eventual realization
Vol reflexivity: short vol selling -> lower realized vol -> more selling -> lower vol
Lower vol -> more leverage -> higher prices -> buybacks -> lower vol (self-feeding)
Whether 60/40 works depends on CB inflation attitude. Not physics.
High inflation tail -> CB constrained -> bonds fail to hedge equities -> positive correlation
Deceptively high win rates of short vol attract crowding; tail risk hidden in expectancy
Duration extension forces hedgers to pay swaps; swap rates rise faster than Tsy; spreads widen
Extension spiral: rates up -> prepay down -> MBS extends -> hedgers sell -> rates up more
Carry = coupon - financing; rolldown from curve aging. Break-even = (carry+rolldown)/duration
Different buyers dominate different tenors; structural shifts move specific curve sectors
Strong auctions validate demand; weak auctions temporarily widen yields. Self-correcting.
Duration = first order, convexity = second order. In large moves, convexity dominates P&L.
Basis = cash vs futures. BNOC = delivery option value. Futures cheap by option premium.
10Y oscillates in range until catalyst. Bollinger compression signals imminent break.
On-the-run bonds on special in repo -> lower financing cost -> enhanced carry
Higher ERP = stocks cheaper. ERP near 4.25% = fair value. Below 2.5% = bubble.
Excess returns exist only with moats. 70%+ of firms earn below cost of capital.
Advantages peak at maturity; disruption resets life cycle positions.
Overconfident entrepreneurs + VCs inflate valuations; collectively a fairy tale
If everybody has AI, nobody has it. Competition passes savings to consumers.
Time and volatility keep equity alive even at zero intrinsic value
Every capex boom ends in glut; picks and shovels work until they dont
TGA drawdown + bank loan creation = short-term liquidity signal for equities
TIPS dominated by liquidity premiums; equity multiples and gold provide cleaner signals
Dealer balance sheet -> financing cost vs SOFR -> hard ceiling on risk appetite
QE is NOT printing (asset swap). Fiscal deficits and CB equity depletion are.
Post-COVID survey contamination means hard data outranks soft data for policy
CPI mixes domestic + import prices; GDP deflator is the correct domestic inflation measure
Rising gold may signal falling trust in financial system; shift toward pricing-power assets
VVIX divergence from VIX is a leading indicator of vol regime shift. Vol-of-vol moves first.
Rising IV compresses risk appetite, triggers dealer hedging, destabilizes spot prices
When FX residual (after controlling for rate differentials) hits extreme Z-score, it reverts. Tradeable signal.
Inelastic collar roll flow creates predictable local turning points. Structural periodic mechanism.
Collar roll shifts gamma exposure at specific strikes, temporarily altering the dealer hedging landscape
Foreign selling of USTs reduces structural demand for duration; term premium rises
Capital diversification away from US assets weakens the dollar simultaneously with yield rise
Rebalancing mechanics create flatter skew and asymmetric vega convexity on levered ETF options
Rebalancing drag on levered ETFs is a direct expression of volatility drain (0.5*variance cost)
Market price = status quo + P(change) * (optimal - status quo). Governance reform in EM -> badly-run companies jump most.
55-83% of acquisitions destroy acquirer value. Phantom synergies, risk transference, overpayment for control.
Passive removes active vol buffers. Each buyer/seller amplifies. Tail risk rises as passive share grows.
Flow-driven markets increase importance of dealer hedging as the marginal price-setting mechanism
Credit regime deterioration leads vol regime shift. Track solvency and credit conditions to forecast vol.
Credit regime is the exogenous shock channel that can break the Ouroboros suppression loop
FICC netting reduces balance sheet cost of intermediation. Expands effective dealer capacity.
Sponsored repo via FICC allows non-members to access central clearing. Enables basis trade growth.
3-layer cascade: explicit blows first (weak hands), implicit follows (VAR/gamma forced deleverage), structural last (buybacks halt). All exit through same door.
The pyramid IS the body of the Ouroboros. Explicit + implicit + structural layers = $2T+ short vol.
Preemptive CB action compresses realized vol via Pavlovian mean reversion. Markets embed rescue expectation.
Suppressed price vol transmutes into hidden tail risk and social instability (populism). Cancer patient gets amphetamines.
Moral hazard loop: CB rescue -> more risk-taking -> lower vol -> CB points to success -> more accommodation
Dragon is the implementation of regime diversification: equal thematic weight, lever equally, no prediction required.
The Serpent IS the Ouroboros regime. Secular growth, moral hazard, reflexive self-cannibalization.
Serpent = negative correlation (60/40 works). Hawk right wing (inflation) = positive correlation (60/40 fails).
Sell first-order vol carry (term structure roll, uncertainty premia) to finance higher-order convexity. Makes long vol sustainable.
Conditions (leverage, crowding, liquidity, valuations) determine whether any spark becomes conflagration or brush fire.
Low persistence: sell vol at 30 works, mean reverts fast. High persistence: vol stays elevated for years, short vol bankrupts.
Inflation constrains CB -> Fed put invalidated -> vol autocorrelation rises -> regime switches from low to high persistence.
TINA phase: flows to US, USD up. Sell America phase: flows away, USD down. Long cycle, reflexive, positioning-dependent.
Exceptionalism bid strengthens USD. Reversal (Sell America) weakens it. Correlation break (USD not rallying on tariffs) is the tell.
General direction down but with updrafts. Goods deflation fast, services viscous, shelter lagged. Each CPI disappointment rolls rate-cut expectations forward.
Updrafts prevent the Fed from cutting as fast as growth warrants. Each sticky print delays normalization.
Must classify asset as mean-reverting or trending BEFORE interpreting positioning. Overbought in trend = bullish, in range = bearish.
Crypto turns first (no valuation anchor, pure positioning). Equities follow with lag. Shitcoin rallies = peak FOMO.
G4+ divergence creates cross-market rate spread opportunities. Front-end divergence persists; long-end bounded by correlated global TP.
Front-end = CB path. Belly = terminal + convexity. Long-end = risk premium + supply + cross-border. Different forces, different timescales.
Variable-rate economies (UK, AUS, CA) crack first from rate hikes. US insulated by fixed-rate mortgages. CB divergence trade.
Rate sensitivity divergence across economies creates FX mean-reversion opportunities when weak-link countries forced to cut early.

loops.label: Debt-Asset Reflexivity
loops.constraint: SLOOS tightening constrains private credit expansion
loops.breaks_when: Impulse turns sharply negative (both public AND private contracting) or Omega exceeds saturation threshold

loops.label: Minsky Instability Cycle
loops.breaks_when: Asset prices stop rising; forced deleveraging begins; Ponzi borrowers cannot refinance

loops.label: TGA Thermostat
loops.constraint: Risk policy floor is DYNAMIC (1-week forward outflows), not static 750B
loops.breaks_when: Debt ceiling binding disables the thermostat; Treasury cannot issue new bills

loops.label: RRP Equilibrium
loops.constraint: MMF WAM limit (~60 days) caps bill purchase capacity; structural RRP floor exists
loops.breaks_when: Bill supply drops (debt ceiling) -> yields below RRP -> MMFs shift back to RRP

loops.label: GEX Stabilizer
loops.constraint: When GEX > $1bn/pt, daily ranges compress to ~0.20%
loops.breaks_when: GEX approaches zero; other factors dominate; VEX takes over

loops.label: Vanna Crash Feedback
loops.breaks_when: IV peaks; dealer selling exhausts; new buying arrives at lower prices

loops.label: Ouroboros (Vol Self-Cannibalization)
loops.constraint: CB ability to suppress vol via low rates / backstops
loops.breaks_when: Inflation spike -> CB unable to rescue -> vol shock persists -> simultaneous deleveraging (1987 template)

loops.label: Mortgage Extension Spiral
loops.constraint: Rate move magnitude determines extension severity
loops.breaks_when: Rates move so far that most mortgages deeply OTM -> extension saturates; or swaption hedges absorb convexity

loops.label: Wealth Effect Loop
loops.constraint: Top 10% drives 50% of consumption; loop breaks if equities correct >20%
loops.breaks_when: Sharp equity correction reverses wealth effect -> consumption collapses -> synchronized recession

loops.label: Fear Policy vs The Script
loops.constraint: The Script (correction economy needs) vs Foam the Runway (policy prevents it). Central tension.
loops.breaks_when: Inflation forces CB to not intervene -> The Script plays out -> term premium expansion -> multiple compression -> recession

loops.label: Commodity-Dollar Reinforcing Cycle
loops.breaks_when: Reversal: commodity bust -> EM outflows -> USD strength -> further commodity weakness

loops.label: Stability Breeds Fragility
loops.constraint: Emergency tools dont eliminate the cycle; change frequency and amplitude
loops.breaks_when: ~2yr calm cadence -> blow-up magnitude proportional to prior calm duration -> capital destruction resets risk premiums

loops.label: Competition Erodes Excess Returns
loops.constraint: 70%+ of global companies earn below cost of capital
loops.breaks_when: One company achieves exclusive capability (temporary moat); disruption resets life cycle

loops.label: Narrative Exhaustion Detector
loops.constraint: Signal strength proportional to degree of consensus positioning; noise if balanced
loops.breaks_when: Narrative is genuinely new (not priced in) -> confirming data produces further trend, not exhaustion

loops.label: Special Repo Self-Reinforcement
loops.constraint: On-the-run bonds attract demand -> lower financing -> more demand
loops.breaks_when: New issue replaces current on-the-run; demand diffuses

loops.label: Credit-Driven Business Cycle
loops.constraint: Each cycle leaves a higher Omega floor (debt ratchet effect)
loops.breaks_when: Minsky moment -> forced deleveraging -> credit contraction -> recession -> emergency tools restart cycle at higher debt level

loops.label: Dealer Balance Sheet Liquidity
loops.constraint: SLR is the binding constraint; nettable vs unnettable repos determine capacity
loops.breaks_when: Quarter-end window dressing, reserve scarcity, or regulatory shock exhausts capacity -> intermediation breaks

chains.label: The Script (Inflation-Killing Sequence)
chains.description: Coupon-heavy issuance -> term premium expansion -> long yields rise -> P/E compression -> equity decline -> wealth effect weakens -> demand falls -> inflation dies. Policymakers keep interrupting at step 2-3.

chains.label: Credit Pipeline to Inflation
chains.description: Credit impulse (4-6Q lag) -> profits -> demand -> prices (6-8Q) -> wages (1Q). Full pipeline is 2-3 years. Wages follow, never lead.

chains.label: Credit to Housing to CPI
chains.description: Credit growth -> house prices (1-2Y) -> shelter CPI (16mo) -> Core CPI (shelter = 43% weight). Dominant inflation transmission channel.

chains.label: Fiscal Deficit to Private Savings
chains.description: Deficit spending creates private savings (identity). Those savings go into bills (stimulative) or coupons (restrictive). Composition determines financial conditions.

chains.label: Debt Ceiling to RRP Drain
chains.description: Ceiling binds -> EM depletion -> bill suppressed -> TGA drains. Resolution -> aggressive bill issuance -> massive supply -> RRP drains as MMFs buy bills.

chains.label: QT Transmission Path
chains.description: Fed runoff reduces reserves. If bills: RRP drains (benign). If coupons: reserves drain (bites). Distribution matters: pockets of scarcity trigger stress.

chains.label: Commodity-Oil-Wages Chain
chains.description: Credit -> equities -> dollar weakens -> oil rises -> CPI -> wages. Wages are anchored to oil prices, not expectations. Full mediated chain.

chains.label: GEX Collapse to Crash
chains.description: GEX declines to zero -> stabilization fails -> IV spikes -> VEX turns negative -> dealer selling -> crash. Requires customer-sold puts going ITM.

chains.label: Ouroboros Reversal (Vol Death Spiral)
chains.description: Vol shock -> VAR models force deleverage -> selling -> more vol -> more deleverage -> death spiral. 1987 template. Only ends with policy intervention or exhaustion.

chains.label: Bessant-Fed Monetization Chain
chains.description: Bill-heavy issuance -> bill market approaches capacity -> Fed RMPs absorb excess -> balance sheet grows. Not QE, but expanding SOMA. Implicit monetization.

chains.label: Financial Crisis Sequence
chains.description: Unexpected losses on safe secured lending -> 50-100x provisioning spike -> credit supply freeze -> all sectors synchronized contraction -> deep recession. Requires bank recapitalization.

chains.label: Panic vs Crisis Decision Tree
chains.description: Equity drawdown -> Is credit supply freezing? NO = panic (buy dip). YES = crisis (defend). Most drawdowns are panics. The single most important real-time assessment.

chains.label: Fiscal Deficit to Equity Prices (Full Path)
chains.description: Deficit -> credit impulse -> demand -> profits -> multiples -> equity prices. Multiple paths: direct demand, wealth effect, collateral channel.

chains.label: Cross-Border Capital Composition Shift
chains.description: Post-GFC: bank paper -> equities. Structural bid for S&P. Less UST buying from foreign CBs -> term premium pressure.

chains.label: Claim Dilution to Inflation
chains.description: New debt exceeds productive capacity -> excess claims -> profits rise first -> GDP -> prices -> wages. Unifies credit-to-inflation in one causal chain.

chains.label: AI Wealth Paradox Chain
chains.description: AI valuations -> equity gains -> wealth effect -> consumption -> sticky inflation (short run). But AI productivity -> labor displacement -> disinflation (long run). Opposing forces.

chains.label: Narrative Exhaustion to Reversal
chains.description: Narrative persists -> positioning aligns -> confirming data arrives -> price doesnt respond -> signal -> positioned longs liquidate -> trend reversal.

chains.label: Valuation Decision Tree
chains.description: Tell the story -> convert to numbers -> compute break-even revenue -> apply 3P test -> check pricing vs valuation -> act. Complete investment process.

chains.label: Merton Capital Structure to Credit Spreads
chains.description: Equity = call on assets; debt = short put. Equity vol determines fair credit spread. Vol and credit must reconcile.

chains.label: Dampener Exhaustion Cascade
chains.description: Stabilizer absorbs stress (partial claims, emergency tools). Finite capacity. When exhausted -> hidden distress becomes visible -> potential crisis trigger.

regimes.label: Supported Nominal Growth
regimes.description: Debt impulse positive, deficit large, growth supported. Default regime. Risk assets bid.
regimes.transition_out: Inflation tail rises (->R02), funding stress (->R05), drawdown (->R07/R08)

regimes.label: Inflation-Constrained
regimes.description: Inflation tail high, CB constrained, cannot ease on equity dip. Bonds fail as hedge. Positive stock-bond correlation.
regimes.transition_out: Inflation subsides (->R01), stagflation deepens (->R03)

regimes.label: Stagflation
regimes.description: Both growth and inflation mandates flash red. Fed paralyzed. Both tails expand. Vol spikes. Curve shape uninformative.
regimes.transition_out: One mandate dominates (->R02 or ->R04)

regimes.label: Employment Mandate Dominant
regimes.description: Labor market softening, Fed easing bias, bull steepening. Front end rallies. Wealth effect sustaining but at risk.
regimes.transition_out: Inflation reasserts (->R02), recession materializes (->R08)

regimes.label: Plumbing Stress
regimes.description: Reserve scarcity hits pockets. Repo rate spikes. Forced selling. Not economic -- mechanical. Sept 2019 template.
regimes.transition_out: Policy response deployed (SRF, TOMOs, RMPs) -> R01

regimes.label: Debt Ceiling Binding
regimes.description: EM depletion, bill issuance suppressed, TGA draining, front-end scarcity. Thermostat disabled.
regimes.transition_out: Congress raises/suspends limit -> R07 (refill)

regimes.label: TGA Refill / Post-Resolution
regimes.description: Aggressive bill issuance, RRP draining, liquidity tightening. Bills absorb all marginal funding.
regimes.transition_out: TGA reaches policy floor -> R01

regimes.label: Credit Crisis
regimes.description: Secured lending losses, provisioning spike 50-100x, credit supply freeze. Deep recession. Requires bank recapitalization.
regimes.transition_out: Bank recap + policy intervention -> R01 at higher Omega

regimes.label: Panic / Positioning Flush
regimes.description: Leverage unwind, no credit deterioration. Recoverable. Buy the dip. Impulse still supportive.
regimes.transition_out: Positioning clears -> R01

regimes.label: Low Vol / Contango
regimes.description: IV low, term structure steep, VRP positive. Theta collection favored. Short gamma wins most days.
regimes.transition_out: Catalyst spikes vol (->R11), systemic shock (->R13)

regimes.label: Elevated Vol / Backwardation
regimes.description: IV spiked, term structure inverted, skew steep. Gamma scalping favored. Convexity pays off.
regimes.transition_out: Event resolves -> R12, or persists -> R13

regimes.label: Vol Crush (Post-Event)
regimes.description: Event resolves, IV collapses, gamma expires worthless. Short gamma wins.
regimes.transition_out: Normalizes -> R10

regimes.label: Correlation Spike / Dispersion Collapse
regimes.description: Everything correlates to 1. Index vol explodes. Dispersion collapses. Index puts pay off.
regimes.transition_out: Shock absorbed -> R11 -> R10

regimes.label: Credit Expansion Phase
regimes.description: Delta-Omega positive, profits rising, assets appreciating. Credit-asset expansion loop active.
regimes.transition_out: Omega approaches saturation (->R15), impulse turns negative (->R16)

regimes.label: Saturation Approach
regimes.description: Omega near threshold. Marginal credit ineffective. Growth collapses to ROA. Yields forced down. Japan-like.
regimes.transition_out: Credit contracts (->R16), or policy maintains stasis

regimes.label: Credit Contraction / Deleveraging
regimes.description: Delta-Omega negative. Profits falling. Assets declining. Deleveraging spiral. Deflation risk.
regimes.transition_out: Prolonged -> R15 (debt-deflation), or policy restarts cycle -> R14

regimes.label: Sell-US-Assets
regimes.description: Positive equity-Treasury correlation. Dollar weakening simultaneously. Term premium spiking. De-dollarization narrative.
regimes.transition_out: Treasuries re-establish safe haven via strong auctions -> R18

regimes.label: Flight-to-Quality Intact
regimes.description: Negative equity-Treasury correlation restored. Safe haven bid. Bull flattening. Duration hedges work.
regimes.transition_out: Inflation reasserts -> R02, growth resumes -> R01

regimes.label: Fair Value Equilibrium
regimes.description: Implied ERP near historical average (~4.25%). Stocks fairly valued. Competition erodes excess returns normally.
regimes.transition_out: ERP compresses -> R20, disruption -> R22

regimes.label: Rich Pricing / Pre-Bubble
regimes.description: ERP below average (3-4%). Overvalued but not bubble. PE elevated. Narratives gaining strength.
regimes.transition_out: ERP collapses further -> R21, correction -> R19

regimes.label: Bubble (Big Market Delusion Active)
regimes.description: ERP < 2.5%. Sum of break-even revenues > TAM. Individually plausible, collectively fairy tale. 1999 template.
regimes.transition_out: Earnings collapse / narrative collapses -> R19 or R08

regimes.label: Disruption Regime
regimes.description: New technology resets competitive advantages. Life cycles compressed. Competition turbo-charged. Winners and losers sorting.
regimes.transition_out: Disruption matures, new moats form -> R19

axioms.statement: Credit impulse (second derivative of debt) drives economic inflections, not credit level
Private credit has ~3x the GDP multiplier of public deficit spending
Loans create deposits, not the reverse. Banks do not lend reserves. Credit is endogenous.
Corporate profits are proportional to total debt (Kalecki: Pi ~ 3.5% x Debt), not productivity
Debt saturation (Omega_sat) is the threshold where marginal debt stops boosting growth. Japan is the completed experiment.
QE is not money printing (asset swap). Fiscal deficits are the true money creation channel.
Wages follow prices adaptively with 1-quarter lag; expectations play a decorative, not causal, role
The bond market is everything: all asset classes price off the risk-free long rate
Term premium (not fed funds rate) determines the actual stance of financial conditions
Treasury issuance composition (bills vs coupons) is the most impactful fiscal policy decision when debt/GDP is high
Policymakers follow a fear policy: they will intervene to prevent a disorderly bond market
Protecting bonds (future money) devalues currency (spot money). Cannot save both.
The post-pandemic economy is income-driven, not credit-driven. Short rates are ineffective.
The distribution of rate outcomes matters more than the point forecast
Committee divergence structurally brakes the speed of policy repricing independent of data
Options ARE the order book: dealer delta-hedging obligations dominate index liquidity
GEX stabilizes, VEX destabilizes: crashes are driven by negative VEX, not GEX alone
Volatility is the only asset class: all strategies decompose into short vol or long vol
You cannot destroy risk; you can only transmute it in form
Stock-bond anti-correlation is a regime, not a law of physics. It has failed repeatedly over 120 years.
The modern institutional portfolio is ~98% short vol and does not know it
Price action is the primary information source; narrative is secondary and often wrong
Positioning extremes create fragile equilibria that resolve violently in max-pain direction
The economy is structurally k-shaped; aggregate data hides the distribution that drives prices
The US dollar is the master cross-asset regime variable; dollar behavior encodes the global state
Commodities drive the dollar, not the reverse (consensus has causality backward)
CA deficit = capital inflow (identity). Surplus countries must export capital.
Financial crises require unexpected losses on SAFE secured lending, not junk bonds
Most drawdowns are panics (recoverable), not crises. The distinction is THE most important real-time assessment.
Stability breeds instability: calm periods build leverage that makes the eventual correction violent
The TGA risk policy floor is dynamic (1-week forward outflows), not the static 750B number
Bills are the shock absorber: coupons are slow policy, bills are fast tactical
SLR is the binding constraint on dealer intermediation capacity
Bond price-yield relationship is inverse and convex: gains exceed losses for equal yield changes
Carry is known, volatility is uncertain. Carry-to-vol ratio is the proper risk-adjusted measure.
Mortgage negative convexity is the dominant feedback loop in rates: extension forces selling that amplifies moves
The value of any asset is the PV of expected cash flows. Earnings are not cash flows.
Competitive advantages are the only source of excess returns. Without moats, growth destroys value.
Every valuation is a bridge between a story and a number. Stories without numbers are fairy tales.
AI will reduce margins, not increase them, across the aggregate. If everybody has it, nobody has it.
Every capex boom ends in a glut. AI follows the same arc.
Bond yields track the credit pipeline, not an exogenous r*. No bond vigilantes.
Auctions bring out buyers. Supply concerns cap yields but do not break them.
TIPS are a broken barometer: dominated by liquidity premiums, not inflation expectations
The gamma-theta contest is the fundamental options P&L mechanic. Everything is always about vol.

forks.label: Income-Driven vs Credit-Driven Transmission

views.position: Short rates are ineffective in an income-driven economy. Fixed-rate mortgages insulate. Need long yields / asset prices to slow demand.
Credit impulse IS the engine. Private credit has 3x multiplier. Bank lending restart is the most powerful inflection point.

forks.resolution: Both can be simultaneously true: short rates work through credit channel (which is currently dormant) while the economy runs on income/wealth. The fork is about which channel dominates NOW, not which exists.

forks.label: Bond Yields: Supply-Driven vs Credit-Pipeline-Driven

views.position: Yields are primarily driven by supply/demand for duration: coupon issuance, buyer base, term premium.
Yields mechanically track the credit pipeline (credit -> GDP -> profits -> yields) with >99.999% Bayesian confidence. No bond vigilantes.

forks.resolution: These operate on different timescales. Credit pipeline drives the secular trend (years). Supply/demand for duration drives the cyclical deviation around that trend (quarters). Both are real forces.

forks.label: Volatility: Regime Variable vs Mechanical Quantity

views.position: Vol is a regime variable: the Ouroboros makes vol both input and output. Suppressed vol IS the risk. Long vol is the Dennis Rodman of portfolios.
Vol is a mechanical quantity: gamma-theta contest, hedging frequency determines measurement. Implied/realized spread is the fundamental P&L mechanic.

forks.resolution: Different levels of abstraction. The mechanical view is correct for individual trade P&L. The regime view is correct for portfolio construction and systemic risk assessment. Both are true simultaneously.

forks.label: What Drives the Dollar

views.position: Commodities drive the dollar: commodity up -> EM earnings -> capital to EM -> USD down. Causality runs opposite to consensus.
Interest rate differentials lead FX; the dollar encodes the state of the global system.

forks.resolution: Multiple channels operate simultaneously. Rate differentials dominate at cyclical frequency. Commodity/capital flow channel dominates at secular (10yr) frequency. GDP-FX parity provides the long-run anchor.

forks.label: Inflation Causation: Credit vs Expectations vs Cost-Push

views.position: Inflation is a lagged accounting cascade from credit. Expectations play a decorative role. Wages follow prices, never lead.
Inflation expectations anchoring is critical. If super-core accelerates and spills into wages, wage-price spiral risk is real.
Tariff inflation is a one-time price adjustment, not demand-driven. The Fed can look through it as long as services cooperate.

forks.resolution: Credit is the dominant secular driver. Expectations matter at the margin for credibility. Tariffs are a one-time level shift. All three views are correct in different contexts -- the question is which channel dominates in the current regime.

forks.label: Risk Premiums: Certainty Premium vs Competition Erosion

views.position: Government backstops raise sustainable P/E. Low rates are a symptom, not a cause. Certainty premiums justify higher multiples.
Competition relentlessly erodes excess returns. 70%+ of firms earn below cost of capital. Growth without moats destroys value.

forks.resolution: Both are true. Certainty premiums raise the AGGREGATE market multiple. But within the market, only moated companies earn excess returns. The aggregate masks the distribution.

forks.label: Market Efficiency: Self-Correcting vs Flow-Driven

views.position: Markets are noisy self-correcting mechanisms. Implied ERP is the best gauge. Timing is a losing strategy.
Markets are flow-driven. Dealer hedging, passive investing, and vol-targeting make markets entirely mechanical. Price action is the primary signal.

forks.resolution: Different timescales. Flow-driven at intraday/weekly. Self-correcting at quarterly/annual. Valuation works over years; flows dominate over days.

forks.label: Plumbing: Macro Overlay vs Mechanical Layer

views.position: Reserves, RRP, QT -- these are macro variables. QT drains liquidity. Bills vs coupons determines whether QT bites.
The plumbing is mechanical. Rate corridor, settlement timing, dealer capacity, nettability. Understand the pipes, not the narrative.

forks.resolution: Both levels are needed. The macro view determines WHAT the plumbing does to the economy. The mechanical view determines HOW it does it and WHERE it breaks.

forks.label: AI: Disruption Bubble vs Structural Transformation

views.position: Big market delusion. Sum of break-even revenues > TAM. Individually plausible, collectively fairy tale. Correction inevitable.
AI is structurally disinflationary and transformative. Productivity gains are real. But wealth effect is reflationary short-term.

forks.resolution: Architecture companies (chips, data centers) are protected -- revenue is real because capex is being spent. Product/service companies (LLMs) are where delusion forms. The correction is healthy: three steps forward, two steps back.

forks.label: Gold: Catastrophe Insurance vs Broken Signal

views.position: Gold is a collectible, not an asset. Cannot be valued. Weak hedge against normal events. Only matters for hyperinflation/catastrophe.
TIPS are the broken signal. Gold and equity multiples provide cleaner real-rate and inflation signals than TIPS.

forks.resolution: Gold serves different functions depending on allocation size. As core investment: terrible. As insurance (15-20%): effective against catastrophe. As signal: rising gold may indicate structural mistrust of financial system.
